long_term_impact: Fire fundamentally shaped human evolution, enabling brain development through better nutrition and leading to complex social structures around shared hearths.

vocabulary.definition: The early phase of the Stone Age, lasting until about 10,000 BCE

quiz_answer: Cooking food, which improved nutrition and helped brains develop.

cause_effect: Cause: Discovery of Louis XVI's secret correspondence with foreign powers; fear of counter-revolution. Effect: Radicalization of the Revolution, entry into Reign of Terror (1793-94) under Robespierre, and foreign wars against monarchical Europe.

title: Reign of Terror under Robespierre
subtitle: 1.6 France Becomes a Republic | Ch 1: The French Revolution
era: Ch 1: The French Revolution
cause_effect: Cause: Robespierre and the Jacobins became paranoid about foreign spies, royalist traitors, and internal political rivals. Effect: Led to the execution of over 16,000 people by guillotine, ultimately causing the public to turn against Robespierre himself.
cause: Robespierre and the Jacobins became paranoid about foreign spies, royalist traitors, and internal political rivals.
immediate_effect: Led to the execution of over 16,000 people by guillotine, ultimately causing the public to turn against Robespierre himself.

title: Directory and political instability
subtitle: 1.6 France Becomes a Republic | Ch 1: The French Revolution
era: Ch 1: The French Revolution
cause_effect: Cause: Fall of the Jacobins led wealthier classes to create a weak executive council. Effect: Political chaos paved the way for Napoleon Bonaparte to seize power.
cause: Fall of the Jacobins led wealthier classes to create a weak executive council.
immediate_effect: Political chaos paved the way for Napoleon Bonaparte to seize power.

title: Coup of 18 Brumaire (1799)
subtitle: 1.8 Rise of Napoleon | Ch 1: The French Revolution
era: Ch 1: The French Revolution
cause_effect: Cause: Political instability of the Directory, military failures, and popular desire for strong leadership. Effect: Napoleon became First Consul, ended the Revolution, established the Napoleonic Code, and expanded French control across Europe.
cause: Political instability of the Directory, military failures, and popular desire for strong leadership.
immediate_effect: Napoleon became First Consul, ended the Revolution, established the Napoleonic Code, and expanded French control across Europe.

title: Napoleonic Code and administrative reforms

era: Ch 1: The French Revolution
cause_effect: Cause: Napoleon wanted a unified, efficient, and standardized set of laws to replace France's chaotic regional legal systems. Effect: Swept away feudal privileges, secured property rights, and heavily influenced modern civil law across Europe.
cause: Napoleon wanted a unified, efficient, and standardized set of laws to replace France's chaotic regional legal systems.
immediate_effect: Swept away feudal privileges, secured property rights, and heavily influenced modern civil law across Europe.

title: Mahatma Gandhi (satyagraha, non-violence)
subtitle: 2.6 Key Leaders | Ch 2: Nationalism in India
era: Ch 2: Nationalism in India
cause_effect: Cause: Belief that violent rebellion was impossible, but moral non-cooperation could paralyze the British. Effect: Provided the mass-mobilization strategy that successfully won India's independence.
cause: Belief that violent rebellion was impossible, but moral non-cooperation could paralyze the British.
immediate_effect: Provided the mass-mobilization strategy that successfully won India's independence.

title: Jawaharlal Nehru (socialism, secularism)
subtitle: 2.6 Key Leaders | Ch 2: Nationalism in India
era: Ch 2: Nationalism in India
cause_effect: Cause: Nehru was deeply influenced by European socialism and believed that only heavy state-planned industrialization could pull India out of poverty. Effect: Shaped the foundation of modern, independent India as a secular, democratic republic with a mixed economy.
cause: Nehru was deeply influenced by European socialism and believed that only heavy state-planned industrialization could pull India out of poverty.
immediate_effect: Shaped the foundation of modern, independent India as a secular, democratic republic with a mixed economy.

subtitle: 2.6 Key Leaders | Ch 2: Nationalism in India
era: Ch 2: Nationalism in India
cause_effect: Cause: Bose vehemently disagreed with Gandhi's non-violent methods, believing that freedom must be seized by force during Britain's vulnerability in WWII. Effect: Reorganized the Indian National Army (INA), dealing a massive psychological blow to British military confidence in India.
cause: Bose vehemently disagreed with Gandhi's non-violent methods, believing that freedom must be seized by force during Britain's vulnerability in WWII.

cause_effect: Cause: Nationalists needed to counter the British narrative that Indians were historically backward and incapable of self-rule. Effect: Fostered deep cultural pride through symbols like Bharat Mata and Vande Mataram, uniting diverse groups under a single nationalist identity.
cause: Nationalists needed to counter the British narrative that Indians were historically backward and incapable of self-rule.
immediate_effect: Fostered deep cultural pride through symbols like Bharat Mata and Vande Mataram, uniting diverse groups under a single nationalist identity.

era: Ch 3: The Making of a Global World
cause_effect: Cause: The invention of the steam engine and mechanized spinning fundamentally changed how humans manufactured goods. Effect: Shifted global economic power to Europe, destroyed traditional Indian handloom industries, and drove the scramble for colonial resources.
cause: The invention of the steam engine and mechanized spinning fundamentally changed how humans manufactured goods.

cause: Industrial Revolution created demand for raw cotton, indigo, tea, and market for machine-made goods.

cause: The war drained European treasuries and destroyed a generation of workers.

title: Great Depression (1929-1930s) and impact on India

era: Ch 3: The Making of a Global World
cause_effect: Cause: US stock market crash (1929), overproduction, agricultural price collapse. Effect: Indian agricultural prices fell drastically, peasants indebted, demand for Indian goods fell, but India became a major gold exporter; contributed to civil disobedience movement.
cause: US stock market crash (1929), overproduction, agricultural price collapse.
immediate_effect: Indian agricultural prices fell drastically, peasants indebted, demand for Indian goods fell, but India became a major gold exporter; contributed to civil disobedience movement.

cause: British industrial revolution produced cheap machine-made cloth; high tariffs on Indian cloth in Britain.

cause: Provisional Government's failure to end war or distribute land; Lenin's April Theses ('Peace, Land, Bread').
